message:
feat(fwc): overhaul schema_nav to resolve allOf/oneOf/anyOf composed JSON Schemas

The schema navigation module previously only handled flat object schemas
with direct properties. Real connector config schemas use allOf for
inheritance, oneOf/anyOf for discriminated unions, and deeply nested
compositions. This left the CLI's schema introspection incomplete for
any non-trivial connector configuration.

The rewrite adds:

- Schema normalization: allOf branches are recursively merged into a
  single flat schema before property extraction. Nested schemas
  (properties, items, oneOf/anyOf variants, not) are normalized
  recursively so the walker only ever sees resolved structures.

- Composed property collection: oneOf/anyOf variants are scanned for
  properties, which are merged across variants with per-variant
  availability and requirement tracking. A property present in all
  variants becomes unconditional; one present in a subset gets an
  "available when ..." constraint note.

- Discriminator awareness: when a schema declares a discriminator
  propertyName, variant labels use the discriminator value (e.g.,
  "type=oauth") instead of generic "variant-1" numbering.

- Variant summaries: SchemaField gains an optional variants field that
  lists human-readable summaries of each oneOf/anyOf branch (e.g.,
  "type=oauth: object {client_id: string, scopes: [string], ...}").

- Constraint propagation: SchemaField gains an optional constraints
  field carrying conditional availability notes and discriminator info,
  inherited from parent to child during recursion.

- Richer type inference: union types (type: [string, null]) display as
  "string | null". oneOf/anyOf types display as "oneOf<variant1, ...>"
  with inline shape descriptions. Arrays show item type inline.

- Smarter scaffolding: scaffold_value now prefers default > example >
  first example > const > first oneOf variant before falling back to
  type-based placeholders. A new scaffold_object helper handles the
  object case uniformly whether reached via explicit "type": "object"
  or inferred from the presence of "properties".

- New helper functions: describe_schema_inline, describe_object_shape,
  summarize_object_member, variant_condition_label, discriminator_value,
  merge_schema_values, merge_into_schema, normalize_schema, and others.

- Tests: existing tests updated for new assert formatting. New tests
  cover allOf merging, oneOf/anyOf field collection, discriminator
  labeling, variant summaries, constraint notes, scaffold from
  default/example/const, and nested composed schemas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
